messages.content:
An array is declared as a[m][n] where m is the number of rows while n is the
number of columns. Each element of the array occupies b bytes in memory. Denote the
address of the first element a[0][0] by “BA”. What is the address of an element a[i][j]
(0 ≤ i < m, 0 ≤ j < n) if the array is stored row by row (i.e. row-major)?
A. BA + (i × n + j) × b
B. BA + (j × m + i) × b
C. BA + i × j × b + m × n
D. BA + m × n × b + i × j.
The correct answer is A. BA + (i × n + j) × b

What is the BEST and WORST complexity of quick sort?
A. O(n) and O(n2)
B. O(log(n)) and O(n2)
C. O(n log(n)) and O(n2)
D. O(n) and O(n log(n))
The BEST complexity of quick sort is O(n log(n)), while the WORST complexity is O(n^2). Therefore, the correct answer is C. O(n log(n)) and O(n^2).

Which statement below is WRONG concerning to stack data structure?
A. push() and pop() are two operations defined in stack.
B. A stack contains a sequence of zero or more items of the same type.
C. List-based stack has no limit on total number of items of the stack.
D. Stack is a First-in-first-out (FIFO) structure.

D. Stack is a First-in-first-out (FIFO) structure. 

This statement is incorrect. A stack is actually a Last-in-first-out (LIFO) structure, meaning that the most recently added item is the first one to be removed.

How many comparisons does selection sort make when the input array with size
n is already sorted?
A. O(log n)
B. O(n)
C. O(n log n)
D. O(n
2
)

The correct answer is D. O(n^2).

Selection sort makes a comparison for each element in the array, even if the array is already sorted. Therefore, it will make n(n-1)/2 comparisons, which is equivalent to O(n^2) comparisons.

How many comparisons does insertion sort make when the input array with size
n is already sorted?

A. O(log n)
B. O(n)
C. O(n log n)
D. O(n
2
)

The correct answer is B. O(n).

Insertion sort only needs to make a comparison between adjacent elements to determine the correct position for each element in the partially sorted array. In the case of an already sorted array, the elements are already in the correct position, so no additional comparisons are needed. Thus, the number of comparisons made by insertion sort for an already sorted array is O(n).

. One difference between a queue and a stack is:
A. Queues require linked lists, but stacks do not.
B. Stacks require linked lists, but queues do not.
C. Queues use two ends of the structure; stacks use only one.
D. Stacks use two ends of the structure, queues use only one.

The correct answer is C. Queues use two ends of the structure; stacks use only one.

In a queue, elements are added at one end (rear) and removed from the other end (front), following the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) principle. This means that elements are processed in the order they were added.

In a stack, elements are added and removed from the same end (top) following the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle. This means that the most recently added element is the first one to be removed.

Therefore, the main difference between a queue and a stack is that queues use two ends (rear and front) for adding and removing elements, respectively, while stacks use only one end (top).

The correct answer is B. carpets.

The first while loop pushes each character of the word "carpets" onto the stack in reverse order. So, after the first while loop, the stack contains the characters 's', 't', 'e', 'p', 'r', 'a', 'c'.

The second while loop pops each character off the stack and writes it to the screen. Since the characters are popped in reverse order, they are written to the screen in the original order of the word "carpets".

Therefore, the output written to the screen is "carpets".
为什么第一个循环是以相反的顺序

第一个循环是以相反的顺序将字符推入堆栈的原因是堆栈的特性。堆栈是一种后进先出（Last-In-First-Out，LIFO）的数据结构，这意味着最后放入堆栈的元素会成为最先弹出的元素。

当程序读取单词中的每个字符时，程序会将该字符推入堆栈中。由于堆栈的特性，最后读取的字符会成为堆栈的顶部元素，而最先读取的字符会成为底部元素。因此，当程序进入第二个循环并开始从堆栈中弹出字符时，最先读取的字符会最后被弹出，从而实现了将字符以相反的顺序输出。

对于输入"carpets"，最终输出的结果是B. carpets。

第一个while循环将单词中的每个字符按顺序推入堆栈，所以在第一个while循环完成后，堆栈中的字符顺序为'c'，'a'，'r'，'p'，'e'，'t'，'s'。

第二个while循环从堆栈中弹出每个字符并写入屏幕。由于堆栈的特性是后进先出，所以第一个弹出的字符是堆栈的顶部元素，也就是最后一个字符's'。接下来依次弹出的字符是't'，'e'，'p'，'r'，'a'，'c'，最后是'c'。

因此，屏幕上输出的结果是 "carpets"。
